trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade. Canada exports natural resources (coal, canola, LNG, lumber, seafood) to Japan. Japan exports vehicles (Toyota, Honda), electronics, and machinery to Canada. Japan is one of Canada's largest export destinations in Asia.

diplomatic.summary: Canada and Japan have deepened their strategic partnership significantly in response to shared concerns about China and regional security. Both are committed to the rules-based international order, liberal democracy, and free trade. CPTPP membership connects both. Regular summits and active diplomatic calendar.

military.summary: Growing defense cooperation driven by shared security concerns. Canada and Japan have joint training and intelligence sharing. Canada participates in maritime exercises in the Indo-Pacific. Both coordinate closely on North Korea and China responses.

financial.summary: Japan is a major investor in Canada. Japanese companies (Toyota, Honda, Mitsubishi) have large Canadian manufacturing and operations. JBIC has financed Canadian energy projects. Canadian pension funds invest in Japanese assets.

energy.summary: Energy is the cornerstone of the bilateral economic relationship. Japan is a major buyer of Canadian LNG (Shell Canada LNG, LNG Canada). Canada exports coal, uranium, and is developing hydrogen exports for Japan. This energy partnership has grown considerably in strategic importance.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific cooperation. NSERC-JST joint programs cover quantum, AI, environment, and materials. Growing collaboration in nuclear energy technology. Active academic exchanges.
